title: Biraja Temple
tagline: Biraja Temple — spiritual town of Odisha

overview.short: The Biraja Temple, or Birija Kshetra, or Biraja Mandira (Odia: ବିରଜା ମନ୍ଦିର, romanized: birajā mandira), is a historic Hindu temple located in Jajpur (about 125 kilometres (78 mi)
overview.description: The Biraja Temple, or Birija Kshetra, or Biraja Mandira (Odia: ବିରଜା ମନ୍ଦିର, romanized: birajā mandira), is a historic Hindu temple located in Jajpur (about 125 kilometres (78 mi) north of Bhubaneswar), Odisha, India. The present temple was built during the 11th century CE. The principal idol is Devi Durga, who is worshipped as Virajā/Birajā (Girijā), and the temple gave Jajpur the nicknames "Birajā Kṣētra" and "Birajā Pīṭha". The Durga idol has two hands (dwibhuja), spearing the chest of Mahishasura with one hand and pulling his tail with the other. One of her feet is on a lion, and the other

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Saptamatruka Temple
topPlaces.description: Saptamaruka Temple is Hindu temple in Jajpur in the Indian state of Odisha. Temple is dedicated to Hindu Tantric and Puranic goddesses Saptamatrikas.

topPlaces.name: Chhatia Bata
topPlaces.description: Chhatia Bata or Chhatia Jagannath Temple is a Hindu temple in the village of Chhatia, between the towns of Cuttack and Jajpur in Odisha, in eastern India. It is devoted to Jagannath and associated with Kalki, the avatar of Vishnu.

topPlaces.name: Hoshang Shah's invasion of Orissa
topPlaces.description: The Hoshang Shah's invasion on Jajnagar, in 1422 AD was a bold military expedition led by Sultan Hoshang Shah (r. 1406–1435) of the Malwa Sultanate against the Eastern Ganga dynasty of Orissa in eastern India. Seeking to strengthen his army with war elephants to counter his rival, Ahmad Shah I of Gujarat Sultanate, Hoshang disguised himself and approximately 1,000 of his horsemen as merchants.

topPlaces.name: Jajpur
topPlaces.description: Jajpur (also known as Jajapur) () is a town and a municipality in Jajpur district in the Indian state of Odisha. It was the capital of the Kesari dynasty, later supplanted by Cuttack.

topPlaces.name: Budha Ganesha Temple
topPlaces.description: Budha Ganesha Temple or the temple of Ganesha in his old age form is found near the Dasashwamedh Ghat of Baitarani River in Jajpur. It is very near to Yajna Varaha Temple.

topPlaces.name: Varahanatha Temple
topPlaces.description: The Varahanatha Temple, also known as Yajna Varaha Temple, is a Hindu temple complex, located on the left bank of the Vaitarani River on an island formed by the river, in Jajpur, Odisha, India. The main shrine is dedicated to Varaha, the boar avatar of the god Vishnu.

topPlaces.name: Panasa
topPlaces.description: Panasa is a village in Jajpur Town of Jajpur district in the Indian state of Odisha. Panasa's nearby villages are Ankula, Balighai, Balipokhari, Beherapatana, Bindhan, Korkora, Kusi, Patapur, Kunjabiharipur and Raghunathpur.

items.note: Saptamaruka Temple is Hindu temple in Jajpur in the Indian state of Odisha. Temple is dedicated to Hindu Tantric and Puranic goddesses Saptamatrikas.

Chhatia Bata or Chhatia Jagannath Temple is a Hindu temple in the village of Chhatia, between the towns of Cuttack and Jajpur in Odisha, in eastern India. It is devoted to Jagannath and associated with Kalki, the avatar of Vishnu.

The Hoshang Shah's invasion on Jajnagar, in 1422 AD was a bold military expedition led by Sultan Hoshang Shah (r. 1406–1435) of the Malwa Sultanate against the Eastern Ganga dynasty of Orissa in eastern India. Seeking to strengthen his army with war elephants to counter his rival, Ahmad Shah I of Gujarat Sultanate, Hoshang disguised himself and approximately 1,000 of his horsemen as merchants.
Jajpur (also known as Jajapur) () is a town and a municipality in Jajpur district in the Indian state of Odisha. It was the capital of the Kesari dynasty, later supplanted by Cuttack.
Budha Ganesha Temple or the temple of Ganesha in his old age form is found near the Dasashwamedh Ghat of Baitarani River in Jajpur. It is very near to Yajna Varaha Temple.

The Varahanatha Temple, also known as Yajna Varaha Temple, is a Hindu temple complex, located on the left bank of the Vaitarani River on an island formed by the river, in Jajpur, Odisha, India. The main shrine is dedicated to Varaha, the boar avatar of the god Vishnu.

Panasa is a village in Jajpur Town of Jajpur district in the Indian state of Odisha. Panasa's nearby villages are Ankula, Balighai, Balipokhari, Beherapatana, Bindhan, Korkora, Kusi, Patapur, Kunjabiharipur and Raghunathpur.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Biraja Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Biraja Temple is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Biraja Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Biju Patnaik International Airport, Bhubaneswar (85 km) and the nearest railway station is Bhubaneswar Railway Station (82 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Biraja Temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Biraja Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Biraja Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Biraja Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Biraja Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Saptamatruka Temple, Chhatia Bata, Hoshang Shah's invasion of Orissa, Jajpur, Budha Ganesha Temple.
